content:
Given below are two statements.

Statement I - Root hair is composed of two layers.

Statement II - Outer layer of root hair cell wall is composed of cellulose and inner layer is made up of pectin.

In light of above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the option given below.

options.content: Both statement I and statement II are correct.
Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct and statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect and statement II is correct.

explanation:
1. Root hair is an extension of an epidermal cell and its cell wall has two distinct layers.

  2. According to NCERT (Unit on “Plant Kingdom” and “Morphology of Flowering Plants”):

    • Inner layer (next to the protoplast) consists of cellulose microfibrils for strength.

    • Outer layer (facing the soil) is rich in pectic substances, making it extensible.

  3. Statement I says: “Root hair is composed of two layers.” – Correct.

  4. Statement II says: “Outer layer … is composed of cellulose and inner layer … of pectin.” – This is the reverse of the NCERT description, so it is incorrect.

Answer: Option C (Statement I is correct and Statement II is incorrect).
